extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out -> not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Proof by considering the only ways the two relevant variables change. The variable `sent` is set to true ONLY by transition t2 (`send_attempt if not opted_out: sent := true`); every other transition (t1, t3-t20, t21) either sets sent := false or, in the case of t2's complement t21, also forces sent := false. The variable `opted_out` is set to true ONLY by t1 (`optout: opted_out := true, sent := false`), and no transition ever resets opted_out back to false. Critically, t1 sets sent := false in the same atomic step it sets opted_out := true, so the instant opted_out becomes true, sent is forced false. After opted_out is true, the only enabler of sent := true (t2) is permanently disabled because its guard `not opted_out` can never again be satisfied; the only send_attempt transition available is t21, which sets sent := false. All key_* transitions also set sent := false. Therefore, in every reachable state with opted_out = true, sent is false. Inductive invariant maintained, no violation within (or beyond) the 9-event bound.
